Luminescent Staff  
 

Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Charges: Unlimited
Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 33
DMG: 13 Dmg Bonus: 126
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +7 HP: +35 MANA: +35
Effect: Lightning Strike (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 10.0) at Level 46
WT: 8.5 Size: LARGE
Class: DRU
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

# Feb 06 2004 at 11:40 AM Rating: Decent

1 post
Everyone is talking about this staff for quadding, I'm thinking it would make a good group item too. I duo with a ranger. On a Drogmor I dould click the staff during fight, on a mount that means I'm medding and doing free damage. Throw a DOT, snare and DS in the beggining, I should be FM at the end by just clicking the staff during the fight.

# Apr 04 2003 at 9:10 PM Rating: Decent
Valdon
674 posts
solo him? By: Valdon,
361 posts
Posted @ Fri, Apr 4th 9:06 PM 2003 Score: Decent[2.61]
As we get older, and more... and more disgusting gear drops. We are able to solo mobs that used to take a whole group, or... and with charming sometimes even more then 2 groups can take. I have a 39 druid, I know I cant take this guy yet... but is there anything thats charmable (animal) in the zone... and can he be soloed when your looking at lvl 50->65? I have a very high lvl druid that would most likely be excited to help be farm this guy. The reason is, at my current level this staff is worth my halfling weight in gold. The idea of a mana-less quad strike, +7 wis... is just disgusting - please ANY info on takign this guy? as opposed to debating grammar errors?

Valdon of Terris-Thule.

# Dec 11 2002 at 6:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591777846330
124 posts
What items/spells/whatever will reduce the cast time for this staff? I tried the Naki Studded Ring which works for my memorized spell but not this staff.

Edited, Thu Dec 12 14:53:34 2002
▲ Page top
RE: cast time
QuoteReply
# Feb 21 2003 at 5:10 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591754794824
10 posts
No item will reduce the cast time of a clicky item... You can however get an extended range II+ item to make the range bigger if you are having problems getting it off in time.
Kilodar Thunderwolf <The Nameless of Mercy>

my stick
QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2002 at 7:19 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591770429855
6 posts
last night in OOT i got one of them off a druid for 3k and a item or 2 went back to OOT with my druid and started the septers at 46 with sow and regan + a few items to lower that cast time i was getting 1 and a half blue a kill off 4 septers got to thinking my cast time was down to 5 i think and i went to the cyclops island and started them and OMG i know my 55 beastlord was getting good exp off them but 2 and a half blue a kill not bad to a halfling druid i think. i told the guy i got it from that i had got a PC on it for 10k and he wanted it back but to bad done mine for EVER! i will use this thing to 60+

Nerfed for Druid only?
QuoteReply
# Aug 28 2002 at 8:29 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758366836
8 posts
I dont know if this was posted earlier...I tried to read all the previous posts but I got bored so heres my scoop, just checked em out in bazaar, says Druid only. I suppose this was nerfed sometime......oh yeah.....this would SUCK for a monk.
▲ Page top
RE: Nerfed for Druid only?
QuoteReply
# Oct 10 2002 at 5:31 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758129715
Sage
104 posts
Was always druid only. When beastlords were introudced, it appeared for a little bit that almost all druid armor had BST appended to it, and allakhazam made that assumption to help jumpstart the introduction of BST gear into the database. Over time, those items which were never actually changed are slowly being corrected, as this should be.
▲ Page top
Extended Range 3
QuoteReply
# Aug 12 2002 at 12:15 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252108299
Scholar
73 posts
With Extended Range you can just barely get off a cast without getting hit. Before they put in the focus items I never really did any serious quading with the staff since the cast time was so long that the mobs would always reach me before it went off. If you try it, watch out, because it will be very, very close, but in the end it will generally work.
▲ Page top
RE: Extended Range 3
QuoteReply
# Feb 23 2003 at 6:23 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591758477457
68 posts
It depends on what mobs you are fighting as different mobs move at different speeds. I used this to with very good effect to quad kite spirocs in tim deep and ulthorks in EW, but found that it was cutting it too close for comfort for wyverns in CS and for Giants everywhere ( i tried the ones at the fort in GD no go they move too fast) and was also too long a casting time to try quading most animals like the various ones in EW and the direwolves in GD. just depends on the mob speed.
▲ Page top
RE: Extended Range 3
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2003 at 4:51 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778323867
View User Forum
24 posts
Actually, and Extended Range 2 item will work with this staff as the spell is below lvl 44.

Also, distance from the mob when the spell fires using an Extended Range item varies depending on the mobs your fighting.

I routinely used this staff to quad wyverns, and as long as I had my moonstone ring equipped, I had plenty of time to get running again before getting hit. WITHOUT the ring was another story, I had to make sure I started moving as soon as the cast bar hit zero or I was feelin the hurt.

Also, a friendly tip, that will add fuel to the "Druids Suck!" argument, with KEI, using Lightning Blast and Ensnare, assuming there were enough wyverns up, I could quad one set of wyverns with Lightning Blast, grab another set, quad them with Lightning Blast, have enough mana left over to completely ensnare another set, use the staff to kill off another round of 4 (yes ensnare will last long enough if you have your head on straight) and have regened enough mana during the third quad to ensnare and Lightning Blast another set.

What's so great about this? If you can hit CS during off peak times (I frequently play there at 3 AM PST), you can quad everything thats up without ever having to sit down to med. The zone will start repopping as your finishing up with the last set.



Edited, Wed Jan 8 16:31:36 2003
▲ Page top
Raptors w/ Lumi Staff
QuoteReply
# Aug 09 2002 at 11:47 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253889253
3 posts
My druid was given this staff a long time ago and I never really used it. Last night, while quadding raptors in TD, I decided to try it out.

Facts: Works nicely just takes forever to kill them with the Staff alone. One "normal" quad leaves me at 40m. With KEI and Lumi Staff (to regain mana in between casts), I was quadding large and small, with no hassles.
Just make sure, if you u attempt using the Staff, you are out of casting range to begin with. Start clicking and eventually the staff will cast. Staff will fire off with the raptors about two steps away from hitting you. Then RUN! lol

Timmayh 53 Warrior
Acin 56 Druid
Aegis Of Unity
Torvonnilous

__DEL__1591770753025
29 posts
GOD I LOVE THIS STAFF. I traded a glowing black stone for it on Povar. No mana cost right click lightening strike. I can quad ulthorks without hardly ever going OOM with this OMG this is an awesome staff. I sacrifice darkelf necros to you Tunare thank you for this AWESOME staff. /em prostrates himself before his god in eternal gratitude.
▲ Page top
Great Stick Heres why : )
QuoteReply
# Mar 05 2002 at 6:48 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592252895988
6 posts
Hello everyone :)

Well I bought this Staff on Karana for 4366pp.
All the money I had at the time lol.
I am a druid lvl 50 I had to sacrafice 15 wis to wear this wepon (Testament of vaneer +10 and Dark ember + 5)the following account will 100 fold justify why it is a Great Stick :)

First Night I killed 57 Ulthorks in eastern wastes non stop with out going OOM in fact I never got lower than 50% mana.

The Ulthorks I killed drop 5 tusks and I bought 1 for 40pp and got given 2 from a vahshir who needed help looting a griffin egg.

I handed my tusks to the cheif and Got 1 blue diamond and 1 Star Ruby Earring so right there on my first go I paid for my stick.

Back to the non stop action I have worked out a trick with the staff in relation to the slow cast time.

At first the mobs would hit me (from the time they were in range till the time the lightning hit about 10 seconds)and 4 angry ulthorks would sometimes strip a bubble of red off me. OUCH

But my roommate was watching and she said can I have a go I think I can see a way not to get hit.

Her first go she was hit (I thought nahh there is no way not to get hit) THEN she pulled it off a direct hit 4 x 184 and no hits to herself WOW.

I said do it again.
She did and again no loss of health.

Then she explained what she saw from a 3rd persons perspective.

The cast bar has a graphic on the first and the last bubble.

Run far enough away from the mob till when your facing them and rapidly clicking your mouse button it says your target is out of range.

Once the spell starts to cast keep your eyes on the bar and as soon as the bar drains to the last buble get ready to move back wards.

As soon as the buble drains equal to the graphic on the last bubble(about 1/5 of a bubble)run backwards the spell still gets off and you don't get hit works everytime with Ulthorks.

It will take some practice but yes you can get away without being hit.

Other mobs I have quad kited are
Wooly Rhinos (can not do this without being hit
Ulthork Man-o-wars Yes but there a long fight
Wyverns in cobalt scars Yes killed with out getting hit 4 wyverns = 1 blue xp bub for 50 Dru :P

This weapon ROCKS its worth 20kpp in my mind

Tonight I will quad kite the Dorfs in BB and let you all know what happened with them. If its doable youl see me killing 4 guardsa at a time in and around Akanon lol XP for ME :P

Sanyareena Soulsister

▲ Page top
RE: Great Stick Heres why : )
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2002 at 11:43 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591752584201
67 posts
Nice post, didn't even consider taking down ulthorks nice and easy with this. The staff is also great if you're quadding something and run out of mana before the last blast. Just run them around a bit more firing off the staff and no more worries. :)

Jeeze, quit tring to be so literal and looking for reasons to flame.

BTW, I want this staff. Mana free snare, dot, regrowth, and now ae, what more could a druid want (beside fewer druids to compete with /jk)

QuoteReply
# Mar 17 2002 at 8:15 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361916697
19 posts
Um, NO ! The Lumi Staff has a RIGHT CLICKABLE / Mana Free effect ! That means that you RIGHT CLICK it ..... It is NOT A PROC ! You do not melee with it to get the effect you RIGHT CLICK on it. I have been using mine since lv 46. Trust me !

Do not spread false info !

Nerill Wolfbane
Druid of the 56th Season
Drinal

I think I've found the next thing im going to try and buy. ES vambs, Tunare Warders Symbol and this will stop me using mana ever again, well maybe not, but I'd still love to have one of these. I can live with the slow cast and the melee stats of it arent all that bad either.
